problem.title: Contains Duplicate II
problem.description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return true if there are two distinct indices i and j in the array such that nums[i] == nums[j] and abs(i - j) <= k.

example.explanation: nums[0] == nums[3] == 1 and abs(0-3) = 3 <= k. Use a sliding window HashSet of size k: add each element, if already present return true, if window > k remove the oldest.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Sliding Window HashSet [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Maintain a HashSet containing at most k elements (a sliding window of the last k indices). For each new element, check if it is already in the set — if so, two equal elements are within distance k. Then add the element and evict the element from k positions back. This gives O(n) time with O(k) space.
